page: Amazon Web Services c8gb.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 architecture using the Neoverse-V2 model, running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 67 Gbps. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier memory performance, with uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 391.76 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 185.65 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is strong, as demonstrated by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21681.0 and high throughput in Passmark database operations. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression also ranks in the top tier. However, the architecture shows limitations in specific tasks, such as text generation for small LLM models and prompt processing for large 70B LLM models, which fall into the lower performance tiers.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gb.8xlarge offers high resource density and memory bandwidth relative to its compute-optimized hardware class, making it highly efficient for memory-bound compute tasks without the overhead of local storage. The instance is well-suited for workloads such as high-throughput database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and multi-threaded data decompression. It is less optimal for large-scale LLM text generation tasks where dedicated GPU acceleration or alternative architectures are typically required.
description: The Amazon Web Services c8gb.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by an ARMv9 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz, 64.0 GB of memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 67 Gbps. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier memory bandwidth, with uncached read speeds of 391.76 GB/sec and write speeds of 185.65 GB/sec, alongside strong multi-core performance in software compilation and database operations. Conversely, it exhibits weaker performance in LLM text generation. This profile makes the c8gb.8xlarge highly efficient for compute-intensive, memory-bound workloads such as database operations, static web serving, and software compilation, offering a balanced resource-to-compute ratio within its hardware class.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated ARMv9 Graviton cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 67 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gb.8xlarge is a compute-optimized ARMv9 server with 32 dedicated cores, 64 GB of RAM, and 67 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core ARMv9 compute-optimized instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and high-throughput database performance without local storage.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 physical cores at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory and a 67 Gbps baseline network interface
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 391.76 GB/sec read and 185.65 GB/sec write speeds
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and static web serving
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage
